description: Title identifies study as a machine learning screening decision-support model
Structured abstract with objective, data, methods, results, conclusions
Non-invasive screening gap stated; lifestyle-only rationale provided
BRFSS 2015 (primary + natural prevalence); BRFSS 2021 temporal validation if available
All BRFSS respondents with complete lifestyle/demographic fields
Binary diabetes status; Diabetes_binary for 2015; Diabetes_012 (prediabetes excluded) for 2021
CDC-standardised self-report; same coding across years
10 non-invasive lifestyle/demographic predictors; coding dictionary saved
70,692 development; 253,680 natural prevalence; stated in participant flow table
No missingness in selected features; stated in missingness table
XGBoost + isotonic calibration; 5-fold CV; bootstrap 95% CI; Wilcoxon test
Selected on calibrated validation probabilities before applying to held-out test set
7-model CV comparison; best model selected by ROC-AUC; refitted on full training split
Discrimination, calibration, net benefit (DCA), subgroup reliability all reported
Calibration curve, ECE, Brier score, intercept/slope for all evaluation sets
DCA performed for all datasets and reported as figures and tables
Age, BMI, sex subgroups; subgroup n and positive count reported; CIs available
Bootstrap 95% CI for primary metrics; Wilcoxon p-values for model comparison
Training prevalence mismatch; calibration drift; duplicate profiles; same-source validation stated
Non-diagnostic framing; deployment-readiness scorecard framed as research framework only
State in manuscript: publicly funded thesis; no commercial conflict
Code on GitHub/Zenodo; BRFSS data from CDC/Kaggle (public)
